Name: Cafe Africa Uganda
Description:
Café Africa Uganda (CAU) is a not-for-profit organization that catalyzes the sustainable growth of the coffee sector in Uganda. As an independent partner of Café Africa International, CAU is part of a network of like-minded organizations in the major coffee producing countries on the African continent. Café Africa Uganda is also closely affiliated with the Global Coffee Platform, which it represents in Uganda.

We are a value-based learning organization. We don’t solve problems, but create value by facilitating multi-stakeholder consultations, and piloting new approaches for sustainable coffee production for all coffee value chain actors, and especially for small-holder coffee farmers.

We create value by facilitating multi-stakeholder consultations in the coffee sector, and piloting new approaches for sustainable coffee production for all coffee value chain actors, and especially for small-scale coffee producers.

Since its founding in 2007, Café Africa Uganda has been the secretariat of the Uganda Coffee Platform (UCP), making UCP the oldest National Coffee Sustainability Platform (Country Platform) in the Global Coffee Platform network (GCP), and the longest uninterrupted secretariat of any coffee platform in the world.

Café Africa is the recognized authority in Uganda on EUDR and CS3D, and a trusted vehicle for providing information, guidance and advocacy. In its role of secretariat of the UCP, Café Africa collects and collates all relevant information from local and international fora, meetings and networks.

The project “Mahogany Planation in India” is a grouped afforestation activity in form of agrisilvicultural system that intends to satisfy multiple Sustainable Development Goals through Project Activity. The project is implemented on multiple scattered small land parcels within the 65 districts across Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, Karnataka, Gujarat and Telangana States in India. Savannah Global Solutions combines the best technologies from the original business Savannah Forestry Equipment, Inc. founded 1987 in Savannah, Georgia, USA and Savannah Equipment Pty Ltd in Australia to provide the most diverse land development product line-up globally. We are an agritech startup dedicated to advancing African agriculture through data-driven innovative solutions, consulting, and collaborative networks across the food supply chain.

Our mission is to become the digital backbone of African agribusiness by leveraging data, technology, and expertise to maximize operational potential.

By providing a comprehensive suite of services tailored to help agribusinesses streamline processes, enhance productivity, and achieve sustainable growth. 

Our flagship product, AgTrail is Nigeria’s first end-to-end digital food traceability solution, ensuring transparency, quality assurance, and safety across the agricultural supply chain. Powered by blockchain, QR code technology, and data analytics, AgTrail enables farmers, processors, distributors, regulators, and consumers to access verified information on product origin, handling, nutritional content, and sustainability practices.

By capturing and authenticating every stage, from farm to fork, AgTrail builds consumer trust, reduces foodborne risks, minimizes post-harvest losses, and expands premium market access for smallholder farmers. The platform also supports compliance with NAFDAC standards and international regulations, including EU food safety guidelines.

In a rapidly evolving global food system, AgTrail is transforming Nigeria’s agriculture by making food systems safer, more transparent, and more competitive while empowering stakeholders and their products with digital identities.

Description:

We work with agro-buyers and processors to deliver premium, traceable crops, such as seed maize, wheat, and soybean, under forward contracts. To meet volume and quality expectations, 80% of the crops are grown on our own commercial farm, while 20% are produced by smallholder farmers, primarily women and youth, who receive input loans, solar-powered irrigation, training, and guaranteed off-take.

Our model doesn’t just increase yields, it opens stable markets for underserved rural farmers, helping them grow their income and resilience, while ensuring buyers receive consistent, high-quality supply.

What We Offer:
 Contracted production of high-value crops
 Market access for smallholders through aggregation
 Climate-smart farming using solar irrigation & soil testing
 Quality assurance and post-harvest value addition
 Export readiness across SADC and beyond

Impact So Far:
 $55,000 revenue from 15 ha
 100+ tonnes supplied to buyers like Bayer & QBS
 50+ smallholder farmers supported
 Recognized by USAID EDGE and ICA Zambia 2025

Join Us.
We welcome partnerships with agro-buyers, investors, and organizations advancing food security, market access, and climate-smart agriculture in Africa.

Name: AgroGas

AgroGas producerer anlæg som kan indsamle og destruere metan fra gylletanke, for derved at reducere klimabelastningen fra husdyrproduktionen, hvoraf op mod 20% kommer fra stalde og lagring af husdyrgødning.

Med de nye krav i landbrugsaftalen som træder i kraft i 2023, omkring hyppig udslusning af gylle fra grisestalde, minimeres emissionen fra selve stalden, men hvis ikke gassen håndteres fra gylletanken ender den i atmosfæren og forårsager en stor negativ klimaeffekt.

Derfor er det nødvendigt at indsamle metanen og destruere den så effektivt som muligt, således at metanen ikke undslipper til atmosfæren – og her tilbyder AgroGas en ny og unik teknologi til at løse den problemstilling.

Name: Markets and Seeds Access Project in Zambia and Zimbabwe (MASAP)
Description:
MASAP is a collaborative effort of NIRAS A/S, Community Technology Development Organisation (CTDO) and the Research Institute of Organic Agriculture (FiBL) in Zambia and Zimbabwe and funded by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC). MASAP is a 12-year project which will be implemented in three phases of four years each. The first phase will run from December 2021 to November 2025 and will focus on rolling out the project implementation in the four initial districts of Tsholotsho and Mudzi in Zimbabwe and Sioma and Chipata in Zambia. 

MASAP aims to improve the climate resilience and food security of smallholder households (especially women and youth) through a Market Systems Development (MSD) approach, strengthening climate resilient seed and commodity markets in Zambia and Zimbabwe. The project teams in Zambia and Zimbabwe work closely with the local governments and private sector (SMEs, processors, community-owned enterprises, research institutes) supporting the establishment of an inclusive seed and commodity sector of improved open and self-pollinated varieties of small grains (sorghum and millets) and legumes (cowpeas and groundnuts). Bottlenecks in the enabling policy environment are addressed through support to the seed institutions and ministries in the respective countries. Through grant funds and service provision contracts, MASAP facilitates  market systems interventions to target the bottlenecks and challenges for farmers to access seeds and seize the potential of commodity and food markets. 

MASAP is promoting innovative, agro-ecologically sensitive practices that ensure mitigation and adaptation to climate change by the smallholder farming households.

At VensoGrow, we're transforming how agricultural inputs are delivered, making it easier for growers to farm smarter, not harder.
Our BreezeCoat platform allows seed and fertiliser producers to stack multiple active ingredients – like nutrients, bio-stimulants, trace elements, fungicides and beneficial microbes - into clean, efficient, multi-layer coatings.
By improving nutrient use efficiency and enabling better compatibility of inputs, BreezeCoat helps reduce fertiliser waste and environmental runoff. With synthetic fertilisers accounting for over 5% of global emissions, smarter application matters.

Cleaner. Smarter. Lets grow!

Located in a region blessed with fertile soil, ample sunlight, and pure water sources, our farm is a sanctuary where traditional farming methods harmonize with modern agricultural practices to yield the most exquisite and succulent Medjool dates.

As you step onto our farm, you are greeted by endless rows of majestic date palm trees, their fronds swaying gently in the breeze. The farm stretches across acres of meticulously maintained land, where the golden hues of ripe dates contrast beautifully with the lush greenery of the palm groves. The sight is awe-inspiring, inviting visitors into a world of natural abundance and wholesome agriculture.

Walking through the farm, you'll witness a seamless blend of traditional expertise and cutting-edge technology. Experienced farmers, deeply connected to the land, nurture the date palms with care and dedication. They employ age-old techniques passed down through generations, ensuring that each date is cultivated with love and respect for the land. At the same time, modern irrigation systems and sustainable farming practices are employed to conserve water and maintain ecological balance.

The air is perfumed with the sweet aroma of ripening dates, and the surroundings echo with the gentle rustle of palm leaves. The farm is a sanctuary for numerous bird species and beneficial insects, creating a natural ecosystem that supports biodiversity.

Harvest time is a spectacle to behold. Skilled farmers climb the towering palm trees to carefully harvest the plump, golden-brown Medjool dates. Each date is handled with utmost care to preserve its freshness and quality. After the harvest, the dates are meticulously sorted, cleaned, and packaged in eco-friendly materials, ready to be shared with the world.

Name: Lisi Global, Inc.
Description:
Lisi Global, Inc.—Richland, WA—is pioneering the first commercial alternative to chemical soil-pest control and large-scale ROW vegetation management. Our patented Directed Energy (DE) Technology delivers precisely controlled electrical pulses that eliminate weeds, nematodes, insects, and soil-borne pathogens without chemicals—providing scalable solutions where resistance, regulation, and rising costs are limiting pesticide use & effectiveness.
Advanced from greenhouse to real-world deployment, partnering w/ growers, researchers, and operators across the U.S., DE is validated in agriculture, turfgrass, and now on Class I RR ROW, a major milestone for chemical-free vegetation control in one of land management’s the most demanding environments.
What We Do
We develop, manufacture, and deploy precision electronic systems that target pests and weeds in situ. DE Technology combines advanced energy delivery, rugged crop-specific applicators, and data-driven optimization to improve yields and bolster soil health by replacing chemical mitigants.
Where We Operate
• Railroad & ROW Vegetation Management — Chemical-free treatment of perennial weeds using high-rail applicator platforms.
• Golf & Sports Turf Pest Control — Non-chemical insect, nematode, and soil-pathogen control for golf courses, sports pitches, and turf production.
• Specialty & Commodity Crops — Sustainable alternatives to fumigants and pesticides for perennial fruit, nut, berry, ornamental, and row-crop systems.
Why It Matters
Stakeholders face escalating challenges: resistance, regulation, environmental and worker-safety, rising input costs, and increasing ESG pressure. DE Technology offers a proven, effective, sustainable alternative with strong end user ROI.
Our Vision & Progress
12+ yrs of development, multiple patents, third-party validation, and active pilots across rail, turf, and agriculture, Lisi Global is redefining pest and vegetation management, replacing chemistry with clean, sustainable energy.

Name: Climate Resilient and Responsible Innovations in Potato (CRRIsP)
Description:
CRRIsP is a multi-national project led by researchers and collaborators in Germany, Ireland, the Netherlands, Kenya and South Africa. https://www.foscera.net/en/foscera/projects/crrisp.htm
The project aims to contribute to the knowledge base of the potato value chain by deploying innovative tools and solutions in variety development, seed systems, agronomy and post-harvest losses. The project is founded on the premise that events of climate change e.g. increasing temperatures, erratic rainfall, will lead to new or more severe abiotic and biotic challenges in potato production systems globally. These are likely to affect the crop's entire value chain. The consequences are lower - or even total loss of potato crop yield and the effects will differ in different agro-ecologies. An understanding of the effect climate change will have on potato value chains will allow mitigation steps be undertaken from the perspectives of breeding, agronomy and socioeconomic adaptations.

The research consortia aims to add value on the state of the art knowledge by:
i) analyzing trade-offs and synergies of developed innovations and their applicability to different farming, market and food systems,
ii) deploying innovative tools and solutions in variety development, seed systems, agronomy, post-harvest loss reduction alongside mutual capacity building,
iii) engage in knowledge exchange and dissemination on production, storage, market and value chain related topics and
iv) develop and disseminate training and communication material for different audiences from lab to fork.

This project is part of the programme of the ERA‐NET Cofund Food Systems and Climate that has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No 862555.

The Goal for “Project Grow” is to facilitate NGN 150b ($193.0m) in agribusiness investment through 100,000 smallholder farmers (SHF) and 500 agribusinesses, of which 40% are expected to be women and Youth-owned businesses.

Our primary target groups are;
I. Unemployed or underemployed youths (18 – 35 years) and women who will be supported to establish agribusinesses, engaging in production, processing, and marketing of the priority commodities, through the lead–firm approach. 40000 women and youth are expected to be reached within six years.
II. 60000 male smallholder farmers will also be supported within the period.  
III. 500 Agri-enterprises or MSMEs who will act as anchors/Lead firms.

Our secondary target groups include;
I. Downstream stakeholders, particularly processors linked to a large number of agribusinesses and aggregators.
II. LGA extension agents/representatives and communities strengthened to manage the infrastructures/beneficiaries supported by the project sustainably
III. Private sector operators strengthened to provide quality services as demanded by the agribusinesses and other smallholder farmers.

Name: Farm Service Cooperative
Description:
Since 1930, when FSC began as a petroleum supply cooperative, this organization has been dedicated to providing essential resources for farmers and local consumers. Launching a company during the Great Depression and the Dust Bowl years wasn’t easy, but the cost efficiencies and service FSC offered allowed the fledgling company to survive. Back then, cooperative members banding together was still a novel concept.

Today’s farmers and consumers have good reason to consider the value of mutual investment every time they receive their dividend checks. Only a cooperative rewards its shareholders for also doing business with the company they own. And only a cooperative on a sound financial footing can reward patrons while consistently improving the services it offers.

FSC is not the largest cooperative in terms of widespread locations, yet it measures up against others in terms of return on investment. How does that happen? We remain true to the original FSC mandate to put knowledgeable, dedicated people into the field. The services we provide in each division, including agronomy, feed and energy, continue to grow stronger and farther every year. The connections our employees form enable us to know what information, facilities, and equipment our customers expect from us today and, more importantly, what they’ll need us to provide in the years ahead.

Name: U-MIX
Description:
The Challenge: 
Small-scale farmers are faced with the challenge to produce feed with the nutritional density and quality required to support optimal poultry and pig performance, as they require access to certain essential ingredients (vitamins, minerals, other essential feed additives).

Our Vision:
These inputs need to be imported, are relatively expensive and often have limited useful life spans. Our vision is to enable small-scale farmers to produce affordable, safe, high-quality animal protein in Africa and thereby address the critical challenge of dietary inadequacy amongst Africa’s rural communities.

Our Solution:
Our solution is to provide small-scale farmers with the opportunity to participate in the livestock production value chain by utilising their own crops and the U-MIX premix to produce nutritionally balanced livestock feed, and subsequently protein in the form of meat and eggs.

Through the Nuffield International Scholarship Program, we equip agricultural leaders with international learning experiences, research opportunities, and mentorship. Scholars gain the skills, knowledge, and networks to influence policy, industry, and communities in their home countries and globally.
Our alumni network keeps leaders connected, enabling ongoing collaboration, sharing of best practices, and innovation across borders. NI also works with affiliate, associate, and full member countries to strengthen local programs, expand global reach, and foster partnerships that sustain our mission.
With a commitment to leadership, collaboration, innovation, and sustainability, Nuffield International has supported hundreds of scholars worldwide and continues to grow a global network of agricultural leaders shaping the future of farming.

#NuffieldInternational #AgriculturalLeadership #GlobalFarmingNetwork #Scholarships #Sustainability #Innovation

Name: Green Africa Trade Engagement(GATE)

Welcome to Green Africa Trade Engagement (GATE), where we are dedicated to empowering rural farmers to achieve financial independence and environmental sustainability. Our mission is to lead the trade of agricultural commodities while empowering rural communities to drive inclusive growth and improve their living conditions.
Since our establishment in 2022, after winning the WAICA 2022 Annual CSR Competition in Nairobi, Kenya, we have built a vast network of more than 2000 women across over 60 communities in Northern Ghana, with plans to expand even further. Our ultimate goal is to empower millions of rural farmers across Africa financially, economically, and socially because we believe they hold the key to contributing significantly to the growth of every country.
Our vision is to be the gateway in bringing development to Africa through trade, and we are committed to achieving this by aligning our goals with three of the United Nations Millennium Development Goals, including the eradication of extreme poverty and hunger, promoting gender equality and empowering women, and ensuring environmental sustainability.
At GATE, we value our core values of Green Africa, Environmental Sustainability, and Livelihood before Profit, and we are proud to share our profits with the rural women who are the heart of our business. We believe that empowering them is the only way to achieve growth and success.

Name: Bluettes Saveur Blueberries Farm
Description:
We are representing the main blueberry co-operative from West of Romania, looking for business partners to establish a serious, long term distribution relationship. Our blueberry capacity is as following:
•	2023 - 600.000 Kg (bulk or any types of small packages down to 125g)
•	2024 and after – 800.000 Kg (bulk or any types of small packages down to 125g)
For a better understanding of our business, please allow us to give you a short introduction:
In 2018 we finalized the two blueberries farms in Homoroade, Satu Mare, on about 65 Ha total. (Bluettes Saveur farm and Blue Happiness farm). The farms have around 300,000 blueberry bushes with four different varieties that cover the early, middle, and late seasons. Both farms are equipped with the latest technology computerized dripping system that allows a very precise distribution of the nutrition the bushes need to produce the best quality fruits.
Our harvest begins approximately in the middle of June and lasts usually through the middle of September. We harvest manually and we are very careful to follow the proper workflow that ensures the maximum of quality and freshness. We’ve been certified Global GAP and GRASP since 2020 and we plan to get SMETA and IFS certification for the 2023 season.
The farms are located in Satu Mare county, 40 km from the Hungarian border which gives us a great logistics advantage.
Further, in 2022 we’ve finalized a state-of-the-art Cold Storage Warehouse with variable computer controllable temperature. It is equipped with a very modern sorting and packing system from Tomra, BBC technology, that allows us to pack up to 20 tones of blueberries each day.
The cold chain is maintained throughout the life of the product to ensure the optimum quality and the preservation of flavors and nutritional value of the product. This modern blueberry packing facility has been strategically designed to maximize the returns while meeting the most exigent packing and shipping needs of the retail customers.

Name: EcoForce Global
Description:
EcoForce Global exists to regenerate land, restore ecosystems, and accelerate natural carbon capture at scale. We are a global tree-planting and land-restoration company built on one simple belief: when people take meaningful action together, nature responds.

Our mission is to plant billions of trees, rebuild degraded landscapes, and restore soil health using proven regenerative practices. Every tree we plant, every hectare we restore, and every farm we support strengthens the planet’s natural systems improving biodiversity, water retention, carbon capture, and long-term ecological resilience.

We partner with landholders committed to better land stewardship and provide them with the tools, trees, and regenerative methods that rebuild soil and restore productivity. At the same time, we empower everyday people and organisations to directly sponsor measurable environmental outcomes. It’s a global community united by action, transparency, and hope.

EcoForce Global integrates tree planting, soil regeneration, and restoration science to deliver real, verifiable climate impact, not just offsets. We’re creating living carbon sinks, strengthening ecosystems, and driving practical climate solutions from the ground up.

We believe regeneration is more than an environmental strategy, it’s a pathway to a healthier, more resilient future for our food systems, our communities, and our planet. The work is urgent, but the solutions are within reach. Nature already knows how to heal; we’re simply accelerating the process.

EcoForce Global is building a worldwide movement of people who want to be part of real climate action. Join us as we regenerate land, restore ecosystems, and change the future one tree, one soil bed, one landscape at a time.

Name: Hoosac Valley Farmer's Exchange
Description:
In 1920, the NYS Dairyman’s League, the NYS Farm Bureau Association, and the NYS Grange joined to form the Grange League Federation (GLF). The Federation had hundreds of farmer members in Rensselaer County. In 1925, about a dozen men in the Schaghticoke area got together and incorporated the Hoosac Valley Farmer’s Exchange (HVFE), establishing a store and mill on the site of the Empire Mill and Coal Company, at the south end of Main Street in the Village of Schaghticoke.

In 2021, John Halford sold the business to Maureen and Eric Mayer of Copses Farm in Schaghticoke. He had built a new store a couple of years earlier, but never opened it. The Mayer Family completed and opened the new store, on Madigan Road in the town of Schaghticoke, in April 2022.

The mill now works with farms across the East Coast providing organic, conventional, and Non GMO feed, organic and conventional fertilizers, fencing supplies, coal and wood pellets, agro-chemicals, home goods, and much more. 

The team at the Hoosac Valley Farmers' Exchange strives to provide excellent service and expertise to every customer who we work with, and new ways to support the agricultural sector.

Advanced Sanitization Solutions
•	Electrostatic Disinfectant Sprayers: These sprayers apply an ultra-thin layer of disinfectant mist that adheres to surfaces and airborne particles, ensuring thorough coverage and effective pathogen elimination.
•	HEPA Filtration Systems: Our HEPA filtration systems capture airborne pathogens, providing cleaner and safer indoor air.
•	Dry Hydrogen Peroxide Technology: This technology continuously releases vaporized hydrogen peroxide into the air, ensuring ongoing protection against airborne pathogens.
Comprehensive Pathogen Control
•	Our products are engineered to kill 99.99% of airborne and surface pathogens, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi.
•	We provide solutions that improve indoor air quality by removing contaminants and reducing allergens, ensuring a healthier environment for everyone.

Description:

Our core product lines include drive units, trenchers, post drivers, and auger bits, the foundation of what we do and what we do best. These products are built to work across a wide range of equipment, including tractors, skid steers, mini skid steers, excavators, and mini excavators, without limiting customers to a single machine platform.

What sets Premier Attach apart is our patented Duo-Tach mounting system (U.S. Patent No. 11,280,057). Duo-Tach allows a single attachment to be used across multiple machines with different mounting styles, eliminating the need for adapters, reducing downtime, and maximizing return on investment. One attachment. One mount. Multiple machines.

We focus on practical engineering, robust materials, and designs proven in the field. Every product is built with the goal of increasing efficiency, simplifying equipment ownership, and delivering long-term reliability on demanding jobsites.

From construction and agriculture to land management and utilities, Premier Attach products are trusted by operators, contractors, and dealers who value American-made quality, innovative design, and equipment that works as hard as they do.

Description:
On 9 September in 2013, Developing Agriculture and Consulting Environment Co., Ltd (DACE CO. LTD) was established by its two co-founders who each graduated from the Vietnam National University of Agriculture. DACE is a company aimed at the production, processing and marketing of organic spices.
At DACE we concentrate on developing a selected range of important organic spice products: ginger, chili, turmeric, garlic and lemongrass. 
The main growth area for our products is in Cao Bang with an available acreage of over 500 hectares. Here we cooperate with local farmers and with the support of local authorities, we invest in building the agricultural value chain of organic spices. The Ha Quang district in the Cao Bang province is low-income farming area. The chosen collaboration model, with the help from the local authorities, results in a benefit for all parties involved. 
Together with our farmers we have obtained the organic certifications for Europe, USA and Japan for the cultivation and the processing of ginger, turmeric, garlic, chili and lemongrass.
DACE's organic products have been being exported to Europe, USA, Korea, Japan, Australia, Russia and the Middle East. We are working hard to increase the export for the benefit of our staff, farmers and our customers.

Name: American Bin and Conveyor LLC
Description:
American Bin & Conveyor (ABC) is a leader in the bulk material handling industry, offering the highest levels of experience, knowledge, communication, customer service and innovation. We design and build material handling systems and equipment to exact customer specifications — on time and on budget.

We are conveniently located close to both Chicago’s O’Hare International Airport and Milwaukee’s General Mitchell International Airport. Our 50,000 square foot manufacturing facility was built in 1991 on nine acres of land in the Burlington Industrial Park in Burlington, Wisconsin. This facility also houses our administration, sales and engineering offices.
American Bin & Conveyor’s roots date back to 1942. Since then, we have earned a reputation for equipment fabrication and customer service that far exceed expectations.

In 2008, Turn-Key Processing Solutions (TPS) acquired American Bin & Conveyor. TPS specializes in providing complete turnkey solutions for clients in the mineral processing, mining and bulk material handling industries. TPS offers plant design, engineering, equipment selection and supply, project management, site preparation, concrete foundations, structural steel, complete installation, electrical, automation, training and startup services.

This partnership has been a tremendous opportunity for both companies. Owning ABC provides TPS access to our state-of-the-art facility, allowing TPS to offer high-quality custom fabrication and assembly to its customers. The alliance between the companies has enabled ABC to move into the future, taking the next step toward becoming the most competitive and highest quality source for mineral processing facilities in North America.

Name: Wagner Greenhouses, Inc.
Description:
It all began in 1901 on what is now our Minneapolis, MN location when August Cornelius, our founder started producing greenhouse and field vegetables. The original glass greenhouse covered 4,000 square feet, supporting tomato production. As the facilities expanded, the vegetable assortment also expanded to include cucumbers, lettuce, watercress, radishes, and tomatoes. The fresh produce was marketed locally. Rich and Nola incorporated in 1967. By then, retail sales of bedding plants had expanded into a full-scale garden center. The garden center featured Wagner’s geraniums and bedding plants and was open only during the spring season. In addition to the garden center, flowers and vegetables were sold at the farmers’ markets and through wholesale channels. Rich and Nola’s sons, Ron and Scott, were also being introduced to the business during this time.
As Ron Wagner completed his horticulture degree at the University of Minnesota he introduced the company to a relatively new production technique: growing plugs. Plugs were initially used for internal production, replacing the old open-seeded trays. As Ron perfected his growing methods, several brokers became interested in selling plugs. Wagner’s quickly became a leading plug supplier producing spring bedding plugs, summer pansy plugs, cyclamen liners, cool crops liner and specialty florist pot crops liners.
Over the years, Wagners has made several expansions including the purchase of a 250,000 square foot growing range in Hugo, MN which was converted and redesigned from rose production to growing annuals, perennials, poinsettias and more. A seasonal garden center opened at the Hugo location in 2002.    Another seasonal garden center opened in Bloomington, MN in 2013.
Now a fifth generation company, Wagners has continuously changed its infrastructure and evolved the product offerings to meet the needs of both wholesale and retail customers.

Het team van Circular Values Group heeft al meerdere jaren ervaring in de verwerking van diverse organische reststromen en het ontwikkelen van technieken, processen en oplossingen voor deze specifieke reststromen.
Voor diverse productstromen heeft Circular Values Group reeds een concept met bewezen techniek en anders wordt ze wel ontwikkeld indien deze behoefte daar is. Of u nu producent bent van een organische reststroom of behoefte heeft aan een biobased product, Circular Values Group helpt u graag verder. Vraag naar de mogelijkheden voor uw bedrijf!

Naast het afleveren, installeren en het in bedrijf stellen van een toepassing willen we graag ondersteuning bieden in de operations, proces service en technische service op locatie. Middels remote control en online monitoring kunnen we dagelijks de processen op afstand opvolgen en bewaken.

Name: Marso Agricultural Development Organization (MADO)
Description:
Marso Agricultural Development Organization (MADO) is an organization that supports farmers through all stages of agricultural production. Our services cover every step of the planting and growing process, including:
	1.	Land preparation
	2.	Seed selection
	3.	Seed sowing
	4.	Irrigation
	5.	Germination
	6.	Pest and fertilizer management
	7.	Flowering
	8.	Fruiting
	9.	Ripening
	10.	Harvesting
	11.	Threshing
	12.	Packaging and storage
	13.	Marketing

We also provide farmers with advice and recommendations on:
	•	Seasonal planning and climate conditions
	•	Seed variety selection
	•	Growing climate-tolerant crops, especially in areas with saline water
	•	Techniques to increase crop yields

In addition, we conduct trainings on all agricultural activities for farmers, and sometimes collaborate with universities that offer agricultural programs.

Name: Taginus Innovations
Description:
Taginus Innovations is a modern, research-driven agricultural technology startup dedicated to solving real-world farming challenges through science, sustainability, and practical innovation. Established in 2025 by agricultural scientist and multi-patent innovator Shivam Madrewar, the company focuses on creating affordable, field-tested, and impactful technologies for farmers across India.

At Taginus Innovations, every concept begins with a grassroot problem, and every product is built through a cycle of research, prototyping, farmer trials, and scientific validation. With expertise across agronomy, pest management, biologicals, and low-cost engineering, the company has created a portfolio of patented tools, natural solutions, and sustainable technologies that improve farm productivity while reducing cost and environmental impact.

Our innovations span pest & disease management, biological inputs, practical mechanical designs, and sustainable agri-tools all aimed at empowering Indian farmers with better efficiency, higher crop safety, and improved income. By combining traditional farming wisdom with modern scientific research, Taginus Innovations works toward building a future-ready, eco-friendly, and economically resilient agriculture ecosystem.

Today, Taginus Innovations stands as a growing platform for "Research. Innovation. Impact." driven by a commitment to bring meaningful, scalable, and farmer-first solutions to agriculture and allied sectors.

Name: Core Cool Systems
Description:
Heat stress costs dairy farmers millions of dollars every year. Core Cool Systems maintains core body temperature, preventing heat stress and effectively taking temperature out of the equation.  
Variable speed without a VFD and adjusted automatically according to the barn's temperature and humidity provide the correct airspeeds to maintain core body temperature and keep cows consistently cool regardless of fluctuating temperatures. The EC motor is incredibly energy efficient at variable speeds.
Water is a precious resource on farms, especially in the summer. Core Cool Systems offers a low-pressure, low-water consumption evaporative cooling solution. Research by Dr. John Bernard at the University of Georgia proved that the same amount of cooling is achieved using a fraction of the water of other systems.
Fantastic on-farm results in many different countries prove that the system works. Customers are prepared to share their experiences of how Core Cool Systems has improved cattle health and reproduction and maintains summer production. 
Core Cool Systems provide flexibility to match the growing demands of today's industry, fully automated and set according to temperature and humidity. They are cooling livestock; saving water, power, and time.
The global community demands our industry be more sustainable and conserve water and power. 
Core Cool Systems ticks the boxes.
Don't hesitate to get in touch with Nancy Vander Byl at 1-844-GET-KUHL or nancy@corecoolsystems.com to find out more about Core Cool Systems.

Name: Vijay Cotton & Fibre Co. LLP
Description:
We, Vijay Cotton & Fibre Co. LLP, are a 3rd Generation Family owned Company which has been in the cotton business since 1946. We have now completed over 77 years in Cotton Ginning, Merchandising and Indenting business of Cotton. We are also involved in export of Indian Raw Cotton and Cotton Waste.
We moved to exports of Indian Cotton during 2003-04 season and handle all varieties of Indian Cotton for exports. We are a Government of India Recognised Star Export House. We are prominent suppliers of Indian cotton to most of the major International Cotton Merchants namely Otto Stadtlander GMBH, Paul Reinhart AG, Louis Dreyfus Commodities, Viterra Agriculture BV and COFCO Agri. We are also registered with AQSIQ in China.
We committed ourselves to Sustainable Cotton since 2015 and are Certified Traders of Organic, BCI and RegenAgri. 
We have been associated with Aid by Trade Foundation, Germany, since a few years now and in April 2024 we have entered in to an MOU with them and we have been appointed the Implementing and Marketing Partner for the Regenerative Cotton Standard (RCS) in India.

Description:

Name: Guará Sementes
Description:
Do campo, nosso sustento. Da terra, nossa maior riqueza. Da experiência de uma vida inteira dedicada ao agronegócio, o orgulho de carregar no peito uma herança de qualidade e tradição.

Nossa missão, começou com Henricus Johannes Maria Aernoudts. Somos sinônimo de experiência, qualidade e tradição desde 1977. Nossa história teve início com a produção de grãos de trigo, milho, soja, aveia e cevada.

A excelência do cultivo, é o que torna nossa marca referência quando o assunto é olhar para a qualidade de nossas sementes além da habitual quantidade presente no mercado. Nossos processos, existem para potencializar cada vez mais um trabalho baseado na análise minuciosa de grãos altamente inovadores e produtivos.

Nascemos da paixão pelo campo. Do amor pela terra, pela germinação positiva. Cultivamos, ao longo de uma geração, uma herança responsável com foco no resultado satisfatório de quem planta, colhe e comercializa sementes testadas e aprovadas.

Nossos grãos estão espalhados pelo Brasil afora. Marcamos presença nos estados do Rio Grande do Sul, Santa Catarina, Paraná, Mato Grosso do Sul e São Paulo. Hoje, nossas forças estão alinhadas às expectativas obtidas a partir da excelência da produção e comercialização de sementes de soja e trigo.

Da experiência local à multiplicação final. Do plantio baseado em dados à colheita rica em qualidade e eficiência. A atuação da marca Guará está firmada no respeito ao meio ambiente e na preservação dos recursos naturais. É isso que carregamos como princípio. É isso que compartilhamos em cada relacionamento.

Somos granja. Somos sementes. Somos Guará. Desde 1977 acreditamos que experiência e qualidade é sinônimo de tradição.

Existimos para ser elo de força, inovação e desenvolvimento no campo.

Name: Thomas Sherriff & Co Ltd
Description:
Rooted in Scotland. Powered by Progress. Trusted since 1816.

At Thomas Sherriff, our story began long before tractors roamed the fields — before the first steel plough was even forged. Since 1816, we've stood with Scottish farmers, foresters, and land stewards — through every season, every storm, and every harvest.

From the soft hills of the Borders to the windswept farms of the Lothians, our commitment has remained unshaken: supporting those who shape the land with care, resilience, and pride.

And, since partnering with John Deere in 1988, we carry that legacy into the future — with cutting-edge technology, world-class machinery, and a team that knows your challenges because we’ve lived them too.

We don’t just serve Scotland.
We’re part of its fabric. Its fields. Its future.

Whether you grow, build, mow, harvest, or haul — if you work the land, you’re part of our story.

Thomas Sherriff. Trusted by generations. Driven by Scotland. Since 1816.

Name: ZAMine Service Chile SpA
Description:
ZAMine Service Chile, somos una compañía del grupo Marubeni, nos dedicamos a la importación, comercialización y soporte al producto de los equipos Hitachi para gran minería de cielo abierto. Ofrecemos una amplia gama de productos tales como, palas hidráulicas entre 120t a 800t de peso operativo, y camiones de extracción con capacidades entre las 180t y las 300t de carga. Además, ofrecemos servicios de mantenimiento en terreno (MARC, LPP y AT) y repuestos originales.

Iniciamos operaciones en Chile en el año 2014, tenemos más de 10 años de experiencia atendiendo a clientes de la gran minería local, además tenemos presencia en otros países del continente (Perú, Brasil y Colombia). Nuestro amplio conocimiento en el mercado nos permite brindar un servicio de máxima calidad, lo que finalmente se refleja en la alta confiabilidad de nuestros equipos Hitachi.

También, poseemos otras representaciones, tales como elementos de desgaste de la marca BYG (Española), balde para palas hidráulicas y cargadores frontales de la marca JAWS (Australiana), camiones tolva de tres ejes de la marca TONLY (China), neumáticos OTR de la marca TECHKING (China) y protecciones de cilindros hidráulicos de la marca CHROMEGUARD (Australiana).
 
Nuestro lema es Unstoppable Effort. Extreme Support.

Name: Raptrac AG
Description:
Die Raptrac AG entwickelt und produziert zukunftsorientierte Automatisierungslösungen für die Landwirtschaft – mit klarem Fokus auf Praxis, Effizienz und Bodenschonung.
Im Zentrum steht der elektrische Geräteträger Raptrac: eine vielseitige Plattform, die landwirtschaftliche Arbeitsprozesse vereinfacht und präzise unterstützt – von der Bodenbearbeitung bis zur Ernte.
Dank integriertem Fahrassistenzsystem kann der Raptrac manuell, assistiert oder autonom eingesetzt werden. Über den Dreipunktkraftheber und den integrierten Zapfwellenantrieb lassen sich handelsübliche Anbaugeräte nutzen. Ergänzend bieten wir eigene Anbaugeräte an, um manuelle Arbeitsschritte gezielt zu automatisieren.
Raptrac steht für Technik, die mitdenkt – und für Landwirtschaft, die effizient, nachhaltig und zukunftsfähig arbeitet.

Name: AGRIHOLD GREEN LIMITED
Description:
OVERVIEW
 
AGRIHOLD GREEN LIMITED is a startup agricultural business that aims to fight food insecurity in African nations. We work with farmers across African countries, providing them with comprehensive agricultural training programs to increase their crop yields. Additionally, we offer market place platforms for farmers to buy and sell their produce, connecting them to a wider customer base. Our farm-to-table service ensures fresh and sustainable produce directly from farmers to consumers. 
CUSTOMER PROBLEM 
Our business aims to solve the problem of food insecurity in African nations by training farmers to achieve higher crop yields. We aim to improve the livelihoods of farmers  in African countries by equipping them with the necessary skills and knowledge to increase their crop yields. 
 
PRODUCT/SERVICES 
 
We offer an innovative agricultural training program that empowers farmers to significantly increase their crop yields and improve their livelihoods. We also offer a comprehensive agricultural marketplace where farmers can easily buy and sell their produce, connecting them to wider customer base. Additionally,  we provide an ongoing guidance and support through Our farmer Mentorship Program(OFMP).

It would foster innovation in organic practices, improve market access for farmers, and promote healthy, environmentally friendly food production in India.

Name: ZMJ GLOBAL
Description: Zhengzhou Coal Mining Machinery Group Co., Ltd. (‘ZMJ’) was founded in 1958 and was transferred to Henan Province by the Ministry of Coal Industry in 1998. At present, the company has developed into an important global coal mining technology and equipment supplier, and a multinational auto parts enterprise group with international influence. ZMJ is a listed company with A+H shares and a state-owned enterprise with mixed equity. It currently has three business segments: coal mining machinery, auto parts, and investment. It has 28 branches and more than 17,000 employees in 18 countries and regions around the world. In 2014, the company was listed as a "dual pilot" unit for the development of mixed-ownership economic reform and professional manager system reform in Henan Province. In 2018, it was selected as a "Double Hundred Action" enterprise for state-owned enterprise reform by the State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission of the State Council. From 2019 to 2020, it was continuously selected as China Machinery Industry Top 100 companies and Fortune 500 companies in China.

Name: SARE Company Ltd
Description: SARE Ltd (Sowers Agro and Resources Enterprises Limited) is a Tanzanian company offering expert, cost-effective solutions to advance sustainable agriculture and natural resource management in East Africa. We guide farmers, agribusinesses, and NGOs across the entire value chain, from sustainable production practices and compliance with Voluntary Sustainability Standards (VSS) like Organic, Fairtrade, Global G.A.P., Rainforest Alliance, and EUDR, to certification guidance and strategic market linkages empowering inclusive, climate-resilient growth through innovation and sustainability.

Name: National Agricultural Value Chain Development Project (NAVCDP)
Description:
The National Agricultural Value Chain Development Project (NAVCDP) is implemented by the Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Development (MoALD) and funded by the World Bank (WB). It focuses on transforming smallholder farmers across 33 counties in Kenya from subsistence to commercial farming through increased value addition and market participation.

The project builds on previous initiatives (like NARIGP and KCSAP) and includes key investments in infrastructure, irrigation, access to financial services, and sustainable agricultural practices.

Name: Yellowhammer Genetics LLC
Description:
Yellowhammer Genetics LLC is leading the way in development of diverse and high cannabinoid content plant varieties. With over 40 years of combined cannabis breeding work, the founders of YHG are focusing on solving the growing demands of both an aging population and a polluted environment. Our research is therapeutic focused and we are breeding with purpose and intent to help bring relief to those dealing with various ailments. We are also focused on improving our environment by carbon sequestration, soil remediation, erosion prevention, and creating unique solutions to improve our world's ecosystems.  
We use marker assisted breeding technologies to improve outcomes and speed up the traditional breeding process. We do not utilize genetic modification in any of our work. 
Our catalog includes a wide variety of Type I, II, and III chemovars that are suitable for industrial, commercial, and craft scale operations. Our cultivars can be found as some of the top-performing products in many US markets and we are quickly expanding into the global markets, providing a consistent and well-performing suite of genetic offerings for a wide variety of applications.

Name: Elandsbosch
Description:
Elandsbosch is a citrus, stone fruit, and pome fruit farm situated in the beautiful Waterberg Mountains of South Africa. We are committed to producing high-quality, nutritious fruit using sustainable farming practices that protect the environment.

Our stone fruit varieties include Starpeach 2, Temptation, SG-PE3165, Summersun, and Plagold17. We also grow Novas citrus and Afri-Blush and Afri-Rose apples. Our fruit is harvested early in the season, when demand is high.

We believe that soil health is essential for producing healthy fruit. That's why we don't use herbicides, pesticides, or fungicides. Instead, we rely on mechanical weeding, biological pest control, and mulching to protect our soil and the environment.

We are proud to provide the market with the best possible quality early fruit. Our fruit is delicious, nutritious, and sustainably grown. We invite you to contact us to learn more about our products and how we can help you meet your fruit needs.

Name: JAB Agri Solutions
Description:
JAB Agri Solutions

 Founder | Amanda Burchmann

 Advocate for the Australian Livestock Industry | Livestock Producer

 Simplifying industry compliance & empowering producers  

 Bridging knowledge gaps between industry bodies & small producers (hobby farmers)

My journey began as a young child, droving cattle on the Darling Downs Southeast Qld with my grandparents, to carting livestock with my dad for Fraser’s Livestock Transport to mustering her Santa herd of cattle for their animal husbandry requirements. Now, 27 years later, I am the proud owner of my own cattle, but I have realised that the process is far from straightforward.

As a passionate advocate for the Australian Livestock Industry and through extensive industry research, it has become evident that there is a pressing need for simpler processes of industry compliance and regulation for small livestock producers. 

Many small producers are not declaring the correct information and therefore are not compliant. This is a significant risk to all in the industry and to the small producer (hobby farmer) due lack of record keeping, knowledge and education.

To address this, I propose with the support of industry bodies NLIS, eNVD and LPA the launch of the Small Producer Hub using JAB Agri Solutions simplified technology combined with educational components to fill those gaps. 

The Hub will be a platform where small producers and their employees can go for tools, resources and record keeping strategies and know that they are being compliant via simple processes.

Today I am seeking small producers who resonate with the struggle of compliance and regulations of industry to step up and protect the industry and the lively hoods we live and love. Let’s control our livestock market position by being compliant once and for all.

I’m positive we can ensure the Australian Livestock Industry remains compliant and capable of producing top-quality livestock for both export and domestic markets at top prices.

Name: GIORGI MÁQUINAS AGRÍCOLAS
Description:
Radicada en Fuentes, provincia de Santa Fe, la compañía fue fundada en 1958, sobre la base de un taller familiar armado por los hermanos Santos y Quinto Giorgi, provenientes de Ripe San Ginesio, Italia.

La sociedad que creó la empresa estaba integrada por los hijos de Santos y Quinto, junto a otros inversionistas, y liderada por Alberto Giorgi, Nicolás Trossero, Basilio Giorgi, Roberto Giorgi y Hugo Giorgi, quienes adquirieron el taller familiar.

En 1972, la firma pasó a denominarse Giorgi S.A. y a lo largo de los años, fue especializándose en la fabricación de:
- Sembradoras
- Rastras de discos y multipropósito
- Cultivadores de campo
- Desmalezadores
- Descompactadores/ subsoladores
- Arados de cinceles

El 29 de abril de 2023 y con nuevos emprendimientos y permanentes desafíos, la empresa -manejada actualmente por la tercera y la cuarta generación de las familias fundadoras- cumplió sus primeros 65 años de vida. 

Teniendo siempre como base el legado recibido de aquellos que soñaron con generar fuentes de trabajo y reivindicar los paradigmas de un campo más ecológico y rendidor, continuamos trabajando para brindar soluciones a las necesidades de la agricultura y la ganadería, tanto en Argentina como en aquellos países a los que se envían productos que colaboren con la cadena agroalimentaria.

Name: Edo State Oil Palm Programme Independent Implementation Office (ESOPP IIO)
Description:
BACKGROUND 
Edo State Government seeks to diversify the economy of the state by attracting investments in agriculture, mostly in the crop farming and agro-forestry subsectors. The government through the Edo State Oil Palm Programme (ESOPP) is de-risking investments in cash crops (Oil Palm) by providing available and accessible land, access to markets and funds while ensuring that the cultivation of oil palm is sustainable and contributes to the livelihood of the Edo people. 

VISION
To become the oil palm hub in Nigeria – providing impetus for the development of competitive and sustainable oil palm. 

MISSION 
To engage with relevant stakeholders to determine and recommend suitable land for oil palm investments, ensure peaceful co-existence between investors and host communities while boosting smallholder production in Edo State. 

FUNCTIONS 
 Coordinate, regulate and promote all activities relating to the oil palm industry in Edo state which includes but not limited to land availability, accessibility and possession by investors.

Description:
PurePlant Organics ltd is a company dedicated to forging a sustainable future through innovation in soil amendment. Our mission revolves around the development of a groundbreaking soil amender based on biochar – a powerful tool for both enhancing soil health and contributing to carbon sequestration.

In the face of pressing environmental challenges, it is crucial to recognize the pivotal role healthy soil plays in sustaining life on our planet. PurePlant Organics understands this and has embarked on a journey to create a product that not only nurtures the soil but also addresses the urgent need for carbon sequestration.

Our biochar-based soil amender acts as a guardian for the soil, enhancing its fertility, structure, and water retention capabilities. By infusing the soil with organic matter and promoting beneficial microbial activity, we empower farmers and gardeners to cultivate crops that are not only abundant but also resilient in the face of changing climates.

However, our commitment extends beyond immediate agricultural benefits. The use of biochar in our soil amendment also serves as a formidable ally in the fight against climate change. Biochar, with its unique ability to capture and store carbon for centuries, becomes a valuable asset in mitigating the increasing levels of atmospheric carbon dioxide.

In essence, our soil amender is not just a product; it is a beacon of hope for a sustainable future. By choosing PurePlant Organics, you are not only investing in the health of your soil but also contributing to a global effort to combat climate change.

Let us join hands in this journey towards sustainability, where the choices we make today echo in the wellness of our planet tomorrow. PurePlant Organics is proud to lead the charge, and we invite you to be a part of this transformative movement for a greener, healthier, and more sustainable world. 

Good for the planet, Good for you

Name: Incline Conveyor Operations Australasia
Description:
The ICO Conveyor Belt Arrestor has been designed and developed to catch (arrest) any incline conveyor belt that brakes or snaps due to normal wear and tear on incline conveyor systems. The Conveyor Belt Arrestor is mounted below the head pulley where most belt breakages occur.

The Conveyor Belt Arrestor catches both the top and bottom conveyor belts within 0.3 a second and prevents it from running back down the incline. Both ends are then in close proximity to facilitate easy splicing, reducing downtime. The Conveyor Belt Arrestor is a 100% mechanical device that is designed to operate only if there is a loss of tension in the belt.

In other words, the tension of the belt keeps the device open, and as soon as there is a loss of tension in the conveyor belt due to it being snapped or broken the Conveyor Belt Arrestors snaps shut almost immediately and prevents the top and bottom belts from running down the incline.

The Conveyor Belt Arrestor reduces the risk of injury or fatalities and infrastructures damage due to belt and material rolling back down the incline conveyor structure or underground decline/haulage following a broken or snapped belt.

The Conveyor Belt Arrestor reduces downtime caused by broken belts by up to 90%.

Name: Floria fresh
Description:
Exclusive Representative of Perfotec in Colombia, Ecuador, and Miami

Floria Fresh is a new company in the floriculture and agricultural products industry. Committed to quality and innovation, Floria Fresh is the exclusive representative of Perfotec, a Dutch company, in Colombia, Ecuador, and Miami.

Perfotec specializes in packaging and preservation technologies that extend the freshness and shelf life of perishable products. Through this partnership, Floria Fresh offers solutions for the floriculture and agricultural sectors, including Modified Atmosphere Packaging (MAP) systems and laser perforation technologies. These techniques help maintain optimal conditions for fruits, vegetables, and flowers during storage and transportation.

In Colombia and Ecuador, Floria Fresh introduces Perfotec's technologies to local producers and exporters, aiming to reduce waste and spoilage while improving product quality. In Miami, Floria Fresh focuses on ensuring that flowers from Latin America arrive in excellent condition, offering consumers fresher and longer-lasting flowers.

Floria Fresh is also ready to assist interested producers and exporters in other Latin American countries. The company has started testing these techniques with key players in the flower industry in Ecuador and Colombia. For exotic fruits, successful trials have been conducted with Organic Lemons, and ten other major fruits are currently being tested with a large Colombian exporter.

Although newly established, Floria Fresh aims to have a positive impact by offering these advanced techniques, improving the quality and sustainability of the agricultural and floriculture industries in these regions.

Name: Full Value Agriculture
Description:
CHAMPIONING NEBRASKA AGRICULTURE:

The Full Value Agriculture initiative, powered by Aksarben, extends the Aksarben Foundation’s mission to make Nebraska a better place to work and live. Nebraska’s ag industry has long competed on quality and yield, even while implementing advanced methods of production that make it the most sustainably produced ag supply chain in the world.

Full Value Agriculture pursues two over-arching goals:
•	Assist efficient, sustainable producers to be paid for the work they’re already doing and the improvements they’re likely to implement to stay competitive.
•	Build a body of evidence and data that establishes Nebraska’s practices and performance as the prevailing industry standards.

Aksarben’s Full Value Agriculture Initiative brings together the producers, methods, technologies, infrastructure, and marketing expertise to realize these objectives. When farmers, ranchers and feeders are paid for quality, yield and their methods of production, that’s Full Value Agriculture.

Description:
At Affiance, we’re leading a new era in natural sweeteners with Stevia—a sustainable, zero-calorie alternative to sugar. As the world faces rising obesity and other health issues, Stevia provides a natural solution for healthier lifestyles. Our mission is to empower businesses and consumers as we expand from B2B to B2C, making Stevia accessible to all.

Affiance is a joint venture between Press Agriculture Limited and MGF Trust Australia, covering 14 estates and over 10,000 hectares in Malawi’s Kasungu region, transforming former tobacco and grain estates into thriving Stevia farms—the largest agriculture project in the region since 1978.

Why Stevia?
Health Benefits: Stevia offers a natural, zero-calorie solution to global health challenges.
Sustainability: Stevia’s cultivation is resource-efficient, aligning with sustainable agricultural practices.
Scalability: Our farming model supports rapid scalability to meet growing demand.
Our Vision
To become Africa’s leading Stevia producer, supplying sustainable Stevia to global markets and contributing to a healthier world.

Our Mission
We’re transforming the sweetener industry with Stevia, offering a healthier alternative to sugar. We focus on empowering local communities through knowledge transfer and skills development. As we transition from B2B to B2C, we aim to foster shared success for investors, communities, and the environment.

Investor Opportunity
Affiance offers strong returns as global demand for natural sweeteners grows. With over 10,000 hectares of fertile land, we’re ready for rapid growth. We plan to expand Stevia production across Africa, advancing sustainability and meeting global demand. Investing in Affiance means benefiting from returns while supporting sustainable agriculture and community development.

Affiance as an Employer
Affiance fosters innovation, collaboration, and growth. We empower employees to develop professionally while contributing to our mission of transforming agriculture and health

Name: CRENNIS
Description:
Overview

Crennis India is a natural farming solutions brand committed to restoring soil health, improving crop resilience, and increasing farmer profitability through certified, chemical-free agricultural inputs. Rooted in India’s traditional wisdom and strengthened by modern scientific practices, Crennis works closely with farmers to support a transition from chemical dependency to sustainable, regenerative farming.

Our focus is simple: healthier soil, stronger crops, safer food, and long-term livelihoods for farming communities.

About Us

Crennis India develops and supplies high-quality natural and organic farm inputs designed for Indian crops, climates, and farmer needs. Our product ecosystem supports every stage of cultivation—from soil preparation to crop nutrition and plant protection—without harming soil biology or the environment.

Our product range includes:
• Natural soil conditioners and microbial boosters
• Plant growth promoters and bio-stimulants
• Botanical pest and disease management solutions
• Crop-specific natural nutrition programs
• Regenerative farming and soil revival solutions

Beyond products, Crennis provides farmer onboarding, training, field guidance, and certification support under India’s natural farming and participatory guarantee systems. We work at the grassroots level with individual farmers, farmer groups, FPOs, and agri-entrepreneurs to ensure adoption is practical, affordable, and scalable.

Crennis is built on transparency, traceability, and trust. Every solution is developed with a farmer-first mindset—focused on reducing input costs, improving yield quality, and protecting long-term soil fertility.

Our mission: enable farmers to grow naturally, earn sustainably, and farm with confidence—today and for generations to come.

Name: Dotnuva Seeds
Description:
"Dotnuva Seeds" started its brand activity back in 2003, when the company UAB "Dotnuva Baltic" was founded. After 20 years of successful activity, in order to strengthen the position of the certified seed producer in the Baltic countries, it was decided to separate the certified seed production activities of "Dotnuva Seeds" from the other activities of "Dotnuva Baltic". And so, in July 2023, the company UAB "Dotnuva Seeds" started operating in Lithuania, and SIA "Dotnuva Seeds" in Latvia.

In recent years, the demand for certified seeds produced by us has grown significantly. Currently, the seed factory in Dotnuva, Lithuania, produces about 27,000 tons of seeds per year, which is about 90 percent of our total capacity, so in order to ensure a smooth supply of certified seeds to our customers, we must increase production capacity.

In 2023, we will start building a new seed preparation factory in Iecava, Latvia, which will not only meet the demand for certified seeds in the Latvian and Estonian markets, but will also allow us to reach Latvian farmers faster and guarantee more efficient logistics of goods to Estonia.

We represent and distribute seeds of cereals, rapeseed, corn, sugar beet, fodder, lawn grass and bean plants of the most famous international seed companies. We cooperate with DLF Seeds & Science, Saaten Union, DSV, Maribo, Lantmännen, Syngenta Seeds, RAGT, Rapool, Bayer CropScience, Basf, KWS, Saatbau Linz, HR Smolice, etc.

"Dotnuva Seeds" seeds are sold by "Linas Agro". The main seed markets are Lithuania, Latvia and Estonia. In addition to these countries, our seeds are sold in other countries of the world.

Name: BYRON BEARINGS SA / ΒΥΡΩΝ ΡΟΥΛΕΜΑΝ ΜΑΕ
Description:
''BYRON BEARINGS S.A.'' is a family business, founded by Byron Karampatzakis, who became known throughout Greece for bearings through his long association in this field. He started as an employee in a shop selling TIMKEN bearings, later took over the management of a shop selling SNR, RHP, ZKL etc, and in the '90's he entered - with partners - the great family of SKF as a reseller. Since 1996, in his personal business along with his daughter Eleni, Byron Karabatzakis is now an Authorized Distributor of SKF, directly importing from the SKF's central warehouse in Belgium.
Byron Karampatzakis' experience with bearings over the last 50 years has given him a great depth of knowledge and wealth of experience which he passed on to his staff. Thus, this enabled them to provide the very best customer service by taking orders with a smile, having the "know-how" to search for the appropriate replacement part and offering a speedy delivery.
"BYRON BEARINGS S.A." is an SKF Authorized distributor for the last 24 years. Apart from the main object, bearings, you can find anything that "rotates" around this: units & housings, seals, greases, belts, glues, tools, automatic lubrication systems etc. Products such as STC-STEYR, DUNLOP, LOCTITE, and also ΤΙΜΚΕΝ, FEDERAL MOGUL, INA, KOYO, PERMATEX, MOLYKOTE, NATIONAL, GATES, CONTINENTAL etc complement the quality range solutions to the requirements of each customer.

Name: Ihle Fabrications, LLC
Description:
Ihle Fabrications was started on a family farm in Central Iowa in 2002 by brothers Mike and Brian Ihle. The brothers began fabricating liners as a solution to issues they faced with their combines. Since then, they have recognized the growing need for their products and have expanded off the family farm into a manufacturing facility in Polk City, IA. After six years of putting the products to market, Ihle Fabrications has succeeded in helping thousands of farmers worldwide by providing a more cost-effective option when repairing combines.

As of 2022, Ihle Fabrications became a Certified CNH Reman Supplier. They also expanded their product line to more than just repair liners. In 2022, the company began engineering and building the IF Viper Land Plane attachment for skid steers that aids landscapers, contractors, and farmers with land grading.

Ihle Fabrications is committed to continued work alongside farmers and end-users to help solve problems of the future.

Name: Future Tree Nurseries
Description:
Future Tree Nurseries proudly offers MCT-1, a revolutionary Australian macadamia variety, now available for cultivation in Africa. Developed by the Macadamia Conservation Trust and managed locally by TopNut, MCT-1 sets a new standard in the industry for productivity and quality. Originating from a seedling trial in 1982 and following decades of high performance in Australia, MCT-1 has become an essential variety for growers, with over 1 million trees demonstrating its superior characteristics.

MCT-1 matures early, delivering commercial yields within 3-4 years, significantly enhancing grower profitability with an increase in productivity of up to 50%. This variety is noted for its high kernel recovery rate (40%-50%) and consistent production levels, combining an excellent in-shell yield with exceptional kernel quality. Its adaptability makes it an optimal choice for both replanting and new plantings.

The variety features nuts of moderate size with a very thin and even shell, and a kernel that is uniform creamy white, sized 18 mm to 23 mm. The nuts' husk is thin and even, contributing to the nut's high-quality attributes. MCT-1's tree structure is compact with an open canopy, demonstrating tolerance to severe wind, making it a resilient choice for diverse growing conditions.

Future Tree Nurseries is the gateway to accessing MCT-1 in Africa, offering a unique opportunity for local producers to benefit from this variety's remarkable yield and quality. With no bearing trees of MCT-1 currently in South Africa, we are at the forefront of introducing this high-performance variety to the region, promising to revolutionize macadamia production with its proven track record.

Our commitment to excellence ensures that growers receive the best quality plants, alongside expert guidance on maximizing their potential. Embrace the future of macadamia cultivation with MCT-1 from Future Tree Nurseries, where quality meets innovation.

We’re building the business for farmers by farmers to ensure fair equity for farmers and high quality for value for the end consumers.

Name: Hope Environmental -| Compostable Bags & Sustainable Packaging
Description:
The environmental impact of plastics, including PVC tape, has
become a global concern due to their persistence in the
environment, contribution to waste accumulation, and potential
harm to ecosystems. In response, businesses across various
industries, including agriculture, are seeking sustainable
alternatives that minimize their environmental footprint.
Vineyards, as an essential part of the agricultural sector, have the
opportunity to adopt sustainable practices and reduce their
reliance on conventional plastic materials. The use of
compostable tape, made from biodegradable and renewable
materials, offers a commercially viable and potent alternative that
aligns with sustainability goals and environmental stewardship.
Discover the Future: PVC vs. Earthtape
Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) tape and Earthtape: two paths, one
choice. PVC, a ubiquitous thermoplastic, has diverse uses from
construction to medical devices. However, it's highly criticized for
environmental toxicity and poor biodegradability. It is never
cleared from the soil and leeches forever chemicals - the impact of
which is now known to cause many extreme health issues, let alone
its direct impact on soil health.
On the flip side, Hopetape, born from renewable sources, is made with
a unique and patented bioplastic resin and represents the future. It's
eco-friendly, biodegradable, and boasts a lower carbon footprint.
Fully soil-certified by TUV and compliant with all known global
standards. Hopetape is a USDA-certified biobased product
Make the shift. Choose sustainability. Embrace Hopetape today!
ALREADY IN CA and SA

Name: Lamish Tradings LTD - TA Lamish Seed
Description:
Lamish Tradings Limited is a Zambian agribusiness enterprise established in 2023 and headquartered in Chipata, Eastern Province. The company is committed to transforming smallholder agriculture through the production and distribution of high-quality certified seed, agronomic training, and sustainable market linkages.

Lamish works to bridge the gap between small-scale farmers and commercial markets by offering innovative solutions such as seed loans with deferred repayment, capacity building in best agronomic practices, and guaranteed off-take arrangements. This inclusive model enables smallholder farmers—particularly women and youth—to access essential inputs without upfront costs, improve productivity, and increase household incomes.

With a 1,200MT warehouse and agro-shop facility, Lamish oversees seed multiplication, processing, packaging, and supply of crop protection products. The company operates in close collaboration with the Seed Control and Certification Institute (SCCI) and other key stakeholders to maintain high-quality standards and ensure traceability across the seed value chain.

Lamish’s work aligns with Zambia’s national agricultural priorities and global development goals, particularly the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) 2 (Zero Hunger), 5 (Gender Equality), and 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth). Through projects like the Tukula Farmer Project, financially supported by the Innovation Against Poverty (IAP) programme under SNV Netherlands Development Organisation and Sida, Lamish continues to expand its impact—enhancing food security, creating jobs, and driving inclusive growth in rural communities.

Name: BioEnergy-Solutions Inc.
Description:
BioEnergy-Solutions Inc. focuses on enhancing the availability of decentralized (behind the grid) biomass heat to power for communities and businesses while producing sustainable agricultural and soil improvement products.  

Yellowbike Solutions Inc. took on operational control of the  AACT-GPH September 2019, as the initial step in the acquisition of all Licensing, Trademarked Rights and IP associated with the GPHTM.

Bio Energy Junction 1 (EJ-1), the original GPH is the operational hub managing operations, growth of the technology, and product use in the US corridor.   Energy Junction 2 (EJ-2) near Edmonton Alberta, Canada) will break ground in 2020, commissioning, and full production late 2020 early 2021. EJ-2  will be the operational hub for  Canada. The Licensing, IP, and patents reside with Bio Energy Solutions Inc. wholly owned by Yellowbike Solutions Inc.

The Business Development plan remains focused on community and business owned facilities working as a network to produce products that improve soil conditions, benefit sustainable agriculture, and reduce the power draw on the existing grid.  The Energy Junction upgrades and engineering improvements support a variety of feedstocks inputs without impacting the production of the soil regenerative products and without a sole dependency on woody biomass as the primary feedstock source for future facilities.

BioEnergy Solution’s closed-loop biorefinery process leverages the vast potential of nutrient-rich algae and biomass (waste) to create high-value organic soil amendments by transforming biomass or waste into heat which when blended with sunshine organic soil stimulants specifically engineered for regenerative agriculture. Breakthru closed-loop technology and have a competitive marketing advantage of being carbon negative.

We work with industry partners, such as graders and processors, to ensure that the consumer has a choice of eggs that is of the highest quality standard, and is affordable for all household budgets.

Our Vision
Sustainable, wholesome BC eggs are British Columbians’ everyday protein.
Sustainable: BC Eggs are produced in safe, ecologically responsible conditions in an economically vibrant and transparent industry that supports regionalization,
diversity, and affordability.

Wholesome: BC Eggs are a high-quality, nutritious, locally grown whole food produced to the highest ethical standards.

Name: Kisan Botanix Limited
Description:
Over the years, the consumption of potatoes have increased day by day and so has the usage of quality seeds. Farmers look for a reliable supplier who has a various supply of authentic, disease free seeds. To meet the market demands, Kisan Botanix Ltd. follows an integrated production cycle starting from its laboratory to the production of its certified seeds. Any gaps are supplemented by importing rare varieties. Plantlets are produced in our own laboratory, situated in Dhaka.

The first generation comes from disease free nuclear seed through our net house. This seed is cultivated on the farm for three more years resulting in certified foundation and breeder seeds. Seeds that are produced internally, are ensured of having a high degree of quality control. Quality is ensured and the condition is preserved through a concentrated planting and well supervised harvesting as well as a post-harvest storage.

Grading is carried out in each of the steps. Kisan Botanix Ltd. has access to several reliable climate controlled storage facilities in Rangpur, Dinajpur, Bogra and other Northern Districts to ensure proper storage. To provide the customers with the best possible services, we have developed a specialist marketing team to deal with all of our customers' needs and logistics to deliver our products efficiently.

Name: DRAGON-LINE, L.L.C.
Description:
DRAGON-LINE patented technology, combines the efficiency of surface drip irrigation with the 
flexibility and economics of mechanized irrigation systems. DRAGON-LINE is the Orange dripline tubing co-extruded from a blend of high quality PE resin. The pressure compensating emitters 
are continuous self-flushing, welded to the internal wall of the drip-line, and fully operational 
at eight PSI. DRAGON-LINE Emitter Tubing is attached to Dragon Flex Hose which drags 
completely on soil surface, thus the name “DRAGON-LINE”. As DRAGON-LINE is pulled behind 
the system, the emitters deliver a uniform water pattern across the full length of the irrigated area. 
DRAGON-LINE significantly reduces evaporation, soil compaction, wind drift and wheel tracks.

Name: Black Soldier Fly Leaders
Description:
The Black Soldier Fly Leaders Podcast is a platform built to cut through the noise and tell the truth about one of the most talked-about, misunderstood, and fast-moving industries in agriculture today — the Black Soldier Fly industry.

For years, the story of insect bioconversion has been told in headlines, hype, and glossy sustainability claims. But the real progress — and the real lessons — are happening behind the scenes: in the factories, the feed trials, the funding meetings, and the policy debates that decide whether this industry truly takes flight.

That’s what this podcast explores.

Each episode features open, informed conversations with the people building the BSF sector from the ground up — founders, scientists, engineers, policymakers, and investors — who are navigating the practical realities of turning waste into feed, fertiliser, and viable businesses.

It’s for anyone who needs to understand what’s actually happening in this space:

Farmers and entrepreneurs who want to know what works in practice and what doesn’t.

Investors looking for clear, unfiltered insight on the viability and direction of the sector — beyond the marketing decks and investor hype.

Researchers and scientists connecting laboratory innovation with commercial application.

Policy and government stakeholders shaping regulations that will define how insect protein fits into food, feed, and waste management systems.

Corporate leaders exploring where insects can create real value in their sustainability strategies.

The conversations go deep. We talk openly about failure, cost pressures, market saturation, investor confidence, feedstock challenges, and regulatory barriers — alongside the genuine breakthroughs in nutrition, engineering, and circular-economy design that are pushing the sector forward.
